gaps.title: Appointment aggregate has zero invariants
gaps.rule: Aggregates must protect at least one invariant — an aggregate without invariants has no consistency boundary to enforce.
gaps.analysis: Appointment defines four commands and four events but no invariants. What prevents double-booking the same time slot? What prevents checking in a cancelled appointment? Without invariants, the Appointment aggregate is a data container, not a consistency boundary.
gaps.recommendation: Define invariants. At minimum: (1) No two appointments for the same veterinarian may overlap in time. (2) Appointment status must follow a valid lifecycle. (3) Rescheduled time must be in the future.

resolution.summary: Add three invariants: INV-AP-01 (no overlapping appointments), INV-AP-02 (status lifecycle: booked → {checked-in, cancelled}; checked-in → visited), INV-AP-03 (rescheduled time must be in the future).

gaps.title: Visit aggregate has zero invariants
gaps.rule: Aggregates must protect at least one invariant.
gaps.element: Visit aggregate in Clinical Care context
gaps.analysis: Visit has two commands (OpenVisit, CloseVisit) and no invariants. What prevents closing a visit that was never opened? What prevents opening multiple visits for the same appointment? A visit without invariants is a pair of timestamps, not a domain concept.
gaps.recommendation: Define invariants. At minimum: (1) A visit can only be opened for an appointment in checked-in status. (2) Only one active visit per appointment. (3) A visit cannot be closed with zero treatments recorded.

resolution.summary: Add three invariants: INV-VS-01 (visit only for checked-in appointment), INV-VS-02 (one active visit per appointment), INV-VS-03 (visit cannot close with zero clinical episodes).

gaps.title: Treatment aggregate command sequence not enforced
gaps.rule: Command preconditions must enforce the aggregate's expected lifecycle.
gaps.element: Treatment aggregate in Clinical Care context
gaps.analysis: INV-TR-02 says medication can only be administered after diagnosis, but there's no explicit state machine. Can CompleteTreatment be called before RecordDiagnosis? The invariants describe constraints but the lifecycle transitions are implicit.
gaps.recommendation: Define an explicit state machine: started → diagnosed → treating → completed. Each command is valid only in specific states.

resolution.summary: Accept recommendation with modification — rename Treatment to ClinicalEpisode first (combined with LG-02). State machine: initiated → diagnosed → treating → completed. Each command valid only in specific states.
resolution.structuralImpact: Aggregate renamed to ClinicalEpisode. +1 value object (EpisodeStatus enum). State machine documented.

gaps.title: Billing context has no declared relationship to Clinical Care
gaps.rule: All cross-context dependencies must be explicitly declared with relationship type.
gaps.analysis: The GenerateInvoiceOnVisitClosed policy in Billing triggers on VisitClosed (a Clinical Care event). But the relationship type isn't specified. Is this Customer-Supplier? Conformist? The relationship type determines who controls the contract.
gaps.recommendation: Declare as Customer-Supplier (Clinical Care is upstream supplier, Billing is downstream customer).

resolution.summary: Declare Customer-Supplier. Clinical Care is upstream supplier, Billing is downstream customer. Clinical Care owns event schemas; Billing conforms.

gaps.title: No event for emergency/walk-in visits
gaps.rule: Domain specification must cover all entry paths described in requirements.
gaps.analysis: The requirements mention 'scheduled or emergency visits.' The specification only models the scheduled path. There's no path for an animal arriving without an appointment.
gaps.recommendation: Either add an AcceptWalkIn command that creates both an appointment and triggers check-in, or allow OpenVisit without a preceding appointment.

resolution.summary: Accept option A with modification. Add AcceptWalkIn command that creates appointment with type walk-in and immediately emits both AppointmentBooked and PatientCheckedIn. Preserves the Scheduling → Clinical Care contract.

gaps.title: PatientHistoryView crosses context boundary
gaps.rule: Projections should consume events from their own context or from explicitly declared upstream contexts.
gaps.element: PatientHistoryView projection in Patient Records context
gaps.analysis: This projection consumes DiagnosisRecorded, TreatmentCompleted, and MedicationAdministered — all Clinical Care events. But Patient Records doesn't declare Clinical Care as an upstream dependency.
gaps.recommendation: Either move PatientHistoryView to Clinical Care, or declare a relationship from Patient Records to Clinical Care.

resolution.summary: Move PatientHistoryView from Patient Records to Clinical Care. Rename to ClinicalHistoryView for clarity.
resolution.structuralImpact: -1 projection in Patient Records, +1 projection in Clinical Care (ClinicalHistoryView).

gaps.title: NotifyOwnerOnAppointmentBooked is infrastructure, not a domain policy
gaps.rule: Policies model domain reactions. Infrastructure concerns are not domain policies.
gaps.analysis: Sending a notification isn't a domain behavior — it's an infrastructure side effect. Modeling it as a policy inflates the domain model with non-domain concerns.
gaps.recommendation: Remove from domain specification. Handle as an infrastructure listener on AppointmentBooked.

resolution.summary: Remove from domain specification. Handle as infrastructure listener on AppointmentBooked. Not a domain policy.

gaps.title: Treatment aggregate is a pipeline, not independent commands
gaps.rule: Aggregate command density ≤ 6 (Vernon)
gaps.element: Treatment aggregate in Clinical Care context
gaps.analysis: Although the count is within threshold (4 commands), the commands have strict sequential dependencies. This is a pipeline, not independent commands. Sequential command chains suggest a saga or process manager.
gaps.recommendation: Evaluate whether Treatment should be a saga coordinating smaller aggregates rather than a single aggregate with a pipeline.

resolution.summary: The ClinicalEpisode state machine (SG-03 resolution) adequately enforces the sequence within a single aggregate. The cross-aggregate lifecycle is the saga candidate (see HG-04).

gaps.title: AnimalProfile and Owner — potential over-separation
gaps.rule: Aggregates in the same context that always change together should be a single aggregate.
gaps.analysis: RegisterAnimal requires an ownerId. Is there a scenario where an animal profile changes independently of the owner? If they always change together, they may be a single aggregate.
gaps.recommendation: Keep separated if they have independent lifecycles. If they're always modified together, consider merging.

resolution.summary: Keep separated. Owner lifecycle is independent — owners change contact information, add/remove animals, exist before any animal is registered. Separation is correct.

gaps.title: Context term overlap — 'Patient' used across contexts
gaps.rule: Context term overlap ≤ 3 shared terms (Evans).
gaps.element: Scheduling, Patient Records, and Clinical Care contexts
gaps.analysis: 'Patient' appears with slightly different meanings in three contexts. In Patient Records: the animal's identity. In Clinical Care: the animal being treated now. In Scheduling: the animal with an appointment.
gaps.recommendation: Define 'patient' explicitly in each context's glossary.

resolution.summary: Add explicit definitions per context. No renaming needed — glossary definitions make the semantic boundaries explicit.

gaps.title: Zero sagas in a domain with multi-step processes
gaps.rule: Domains with cross-aggregate, multi-step processes typically require at least one saga.
gaps.analysis: The full visit lifecycle spans multiple aggregates across multiple contexts: Appointment → Visit → Treatment(s) → Invoice. This is a multi-step process with potential failure points. Policies handle the happy path but there's no compensation.
gaps.recommendation: Evaluate the visit lifecycle as a saga candidate with compensation for each step.

resolution.summary: Introduce VisitLifecycleSaga in Clinical Care. Trigger: PatientCheckedIn. Steps: open visit → await episodes → close visit → emit VisitClosed. Compensation: abandoned state if patient leaves without treatment.

gaps.title: Invoice aggregate — no payment lifecycle
gaps.rule: Aggregate must model all stated business behaviors.
gaps.analysis: RecordPayment mentions 'full or partial payment' but there's no payment state model. No lifecycle invariants beyond total = sum of line items.
gaps.recommendation: Define Invoice states and transitions. Add invariant: payment cannot exceed remaining balance.

resolution.summary: Define invoice states: generated → partially-paid → paid → disputed → refunded. Add INV-IN-02: payment cannot exceed remaining balance. Add DisputeInvoice and RefundInvoice commands.

gaps.title: 'Visit' vs. 'Appointment' — semantic overlap
gaps.rule: Ubiquitous language terms must have unambiguous definitions.
gaps.analysis: An appointment is a scheduled time. A visit is a clinical encounter. But the transition point — where 'appointment' ends and 'visit' begins — isn't clearly defined.
gaps.recommendation: Define explicitly: 'An appointment is a time commitment. A visit is a clinical encounter. The boundary is check-in.'

resolution.summary: Add glossary definitions. An appointment is a time commitment. A visit is a clinical encounter. A visit does not require an appointment (walk-ins). The boundary is check-in.

gaps.title: 'Treatment' is overloaded
gaps.rule: Ubiquitous language terms must have unambiguous definitions.
gaps.analysis: 'Treatment' in the aggregate means the full clinical encounter, but in common language it means a single intervention. The aggregate is actually a 'clinical episode' containing multiple treatments.
gaps.recommendation: Rename the aggregate to ClinicalEpisode. Reserve 'treatment' for individual interventions.

resolution.summary: Rename Treatment aggregate to ClinicalEpisode. Reserve 'treatment' for individual interventions. Combined with SG-03 resolution.

gaps.title: Who owns the veterinarian schedule?
gaps.rule: All domain concepts referenced in the specification must be owned by a bounded context.
gaps.analysis: ScheduleAvailabilityService checks veterinarian availability, but there's no Veterinarian aggregate or schedule model. Where does veterinarian identity live? This is a missing aggregate.
gaps.recommendation: Add a Veterinarian aggregate to Scheduling with schedule management commands, or introduce a Staff Management bounded context.

resolution.summary: Add Veterinarian aggregate to Scheduling context. Commands: SetAvailability, BlockTimeSlot. Invariant: veterinarian must have availability defined before appointments can be booked.
resolution.structuralImpact: +1 aggregate, +2 commands, +2 events, +1 invariant in Scheduling.

gaps.title: How does pricing work?
gaps.rule: All domain concepts referenced in the specification must be owned by a bounded context.
gaps.analysis: PricingService 'calculates line item prices' but there's no pricing model, no price list aggregate, no pricing configuration. Where do prices come from?
gaps.recommendation: Define a PriceList aggregate in Billing with pricing rules.

resolution.summary: Add PriceList aggregate to Billing. Static pricing for V1: treatment code → price. Commands: SetPrice, DeactivatePrice. Dynamic pricing deferred to V2.
resolution.structuralImpact: +1 aggregate, +2 commands, +2 events, +1 invariant in Billing.

gaps.title: Medical history — who is the source of truth?
gaps.rule: Data ownership must be explicit across context boundaries.
gaps.element: Patient Records and Clinical Care contexts
gaps.analysis: Both contexts need medical history. Who owns the canonical record? Patient Records (long-term) or Clinical Care (active view)?
gaps.recommendation: Patient Records owns the historical record. Clinical Care reads a snapshot at visit start and writes back at visit close.

resolution.summary: Patient Records owns the persistent record. Clinical Care reads snapshot at visit start via ClinicalHistoryView. Relationship: Partnership (bidirectional data flow).
resolution.structuralImpact: +1 context relationship (Partnership between Patient Records and Clinical Care).

gaps.title: What happens when an owner has multiple animals?
gaps.rule: Scope boundaries must be documented as design decisions.
gaps.analysis: The model supports multiple animals per owner but doesn't handle multi-pet scenarios: sibling discount? Combined appointments? Single invoice for multiple animals?
gaps.recommendation: Document as V1 scope decision. Defer multi-pet billing and scheduling to V2.

resolution.summary: Documented as V1 scope: each animal is independent. Multi-pet billing, combined appointments, and sibling discounts deferred to V2.

gaps.title: VisitLifecycleSaga has no timeout for open state
gaps.rule: Sagas with unbounded wait states create lifecycle debt.
gaps.analysis: The VisitLifecycleSaga transitions to 'open' when a visit begins but has no defined timeout. If a veterinarian forgets to close a visit, the saga waits indefinitely.
gaps.recommendation: Add a configurable timeout (default: 12 hours). Auto-transition to abandoned at 24 hours.

resolution.summary: Add configurable timeout. Warning at 12h, auto-abandon at 24h. Add INV-SAGA-01: visit must transition to completed or abandoned within timeout window.

gaps.title: Walk-in path dual-emit creates event ordering dependency
gaps.rule: Commands that emit multiple events must define ordering guarantees.
gaps.analysis: AcceptWalkIn emits both AppointmentBooked and PatientCheckedIn. If PatientCheckedIn is processed before AppointmentBooked, the visit references an appointment that doesn't exist yet in projections.
gaps.recommendation: Define ordering: AppointmentBooked must be persisted before PatientCheckedIn is emitted.

resolution.summary: Define INV-AP-04: AppointmentBooked must be persisted before PatientCheckedIn is emitted. Both events share appointmentId for causal correlation.

gaps.title: Scheduling context at 3-aggregate boundary
gaps.rule: Context size heuristic — ≤ 3 aggregates per context preferred.
gaps.analysis: Adding the Veterinarian aggregate puts Scheduling at the heuristic boundary (3 aggregates). Currently acceptable — monitor if complexity grows.
gaps.recommendation: Accept for now. If V2 adds room/equipment booking, split into Scheduling and Staff Availability contexts.

resolution.summary: Accept current structure. 3 aggregates is at threshold, not exceeding. Document as V2 watch item with explicit trigger condition.

gaps.title: Can multiple episodes exist per visit?
gaps.rule: Cardinality constraints must be explicit in the specification.
gaps.analysis: A visit may involve multiple clinical episodes but the specification doesn't state cardinality. The saga 'awaits clinical episodes' (plural) but no invariant defines it.
gaps.recommendation: Define explicitly: one-to-many. Add invariant: at least one episode must be completed before visit can close.

resolution.summary: Explicitly one-to-many. Add INV-VS-04: visit cannot close unless at least one episode has reached completed status.

gaps.title: Pricing snapshot rule undefined
gaps.rule: Temporal constraints on data must be explicit.
gaps.element: PriceList and Invoice aggregates in Billing
gaps.analysis: Can prices change while an active visit is in progress? If a price changes between TreatmentCompleted and GenerateInvoice, which price applies?
gaps.recommendation: Define pricing snapshot: invoice captures prices at moment of VisitClosed. Add immutability invariant.

resolution.summary: Invoice captures prices at moment of generation. Add INV-IN-03: line item prices are immutable after invoice generation. Standard point-in-time pricing.
